description_long: This is a two-story wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof covered in dark, weathered shingles. The front facade features a covered porch supported by two posts, and a set of stairs leads up to the main entrance. The upper level has a pair of rectangular windows with a grid pattern, framed in dark wood, while the lower level includes a single arched wooden door and a small window. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, giving the structure a rustic appearance. The roof has a significant overhang, providing shade and protection to the porch area. The overall design is simple yet functional, suitable for residential use in an urban or suburban neighborhood.
description: A rustic two-story wooden cabin with a gable roof, covered porch, and grid-patterned windows. The structure features wood plank siding and a prominent overhanging roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, two upper windows with grid panes, and the main entrance area beneath the roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the arched wooden door, a small window, and the stairs leading up to the entrance, with the porch roof extending to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wooden wall with no windows or doors, and a small section of the porch roof visible on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wall and the side of the porch roof, with minimal architectural features visible.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with wide overhangs and shingle texture
attribute_window_arrangement: Two medium-sized rectangular windows with grid panes are symmetrically placed on the upper front facade; a single small window is located on the right side of the lower level.
attribute_door_placement: One arched wooden door is located on the right side of the lower level, accessible via stairs and adjacent to the porch.
